IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: We are diverting sections of overhead electricity cables and replacing them with new underground electricity cables along with removing redundant equipment. Following a customers request, we are also installing a new Independent Distribution Network Operator (IDNO) substation to the electricity network. To complete the work safely we need to turn your power off. Doing this will give the third party their desired outcome as we have a duty to provide electricity when required, improve the reliability of the network and reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future. This work is taking place in The Drive area.

Doing this will improve the reliability of the electricity network in the future. This work is taking place near St Neots Road.

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Lisa at 16:16 - Thanks for bearing with us today. Our staff have confirmed they have managed to restore power to all but one property in the St. Osyth / Clacton-on-Sea CO16 8 area.

We believed the power cut was caused by an overhead network fault, however after investigations made by our field staff and our control room, we've discovered the issue was due to a fault with our underground network, so we're sorry for the confusion.

If your power isn't back on, we'd still like to keep you updated. If we aren't currently in contact with you, please call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We're sorry for the outage today. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

Sent by Lisa at 15:36 - Hi - thanks for bearing with us, I know a power cut can be very disruptive. We've had an update from our staff that there are now 132 properties still affected by the overhead network fault, as they have managed to safely divert some power, getting the lights back on for 22 of our customers.

We'll keep sending text updates to everyone until all the power to your area is on. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe.

Our engineers have arrived on site in the St. Osyth / Clacton-on-Sea CO16 8 area and are investigating the fault.

You may find your power is going on and off; that is normal for this type of issue and is due to our engineers working to divert power remotely. By doing this, we've managed to restore power to 254 customers. There are now 154 properties still affected.

We'll keep sending SMS updates until everybody is restored. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Sent by Lisa at 15:03.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Lisa at 19:21 - We can now confirm that all power in the Stanhope Avenue ME10 4 area should be back on.

Our staff are trying to find the root cause and fix it permanently, however with this kind of issue it's likely that power will go off multiple times before we're able to find and fix it. For now we have restored your power as quickly as we can.

If your power isn't on or you would like to talk to us, call us for free on 08003163105. We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our engineers have now arrived on site in the Stanhope Avenue ME10 4 area.

As any issues that currently exist on our network have affected our equipment at the substation, engineers will start their testing there. They will then check our network equipment throughout the area to try and identify any problems, before trying to restore power.

Our estimated time frame to restore power is between 23:00-00:00, but if this is not successful it's possible that a cable repair may be needed. This can involve ground excavations, traffic management equipment or specialist teams. As soon as our staff can safely update us, we'll give you the latest news. Sent by Decasta at 18:50.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Donny at 09:17 - I'm texting to say I am glad to report that power to the Clarence Road area should now be back on.

I'm sorry for the continued disruption recently. We believe there may be a fault on our underground electricity cabling in the area, but currently it has not developed to a stage that our staff can pinpoint it. Their tests have proven inconclusive, so for now they have replaced our safety equipment at the substation to get your power back on as quickly as possible.

This may happen again before we're able to fix it, but if you'd like to talk to us about this, or if your power isn't on call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
I appreciate your continued patience this morning. We want to be as transparent as possible as we know having multiple power cuts is disruptive and worrying, so I would like to help explain why we can't always fix a problem like this right away.

Underground electricity cabling is insulated for safety and if a fault develops, these materials can re-seal due to heat passing through them when our safety equipment operates and your power is lost. This covers any potential fault again, meaning our staff cannot locate it using their tests or specialist equipment.

We'll know more once our staff contact us again, so I'll be in touch again shortly.

Sent by Donny at 08:37.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the Clarence Road area. Due to there being multiple power cuts over a short space of time, our engineers are going to be testing if the network is still healthy.

They will start at the substation, and often apply specialist equipment that may turn your power back on for a brief period, so don't worry if your power comes on and goes off again. They will also check smaller sites which run along our cabling route, these break our network down into smaller sections and can help pinpoint any possible problems.

Our estimated time frame of 11:00-12:00 hasn't changed, so I'll let you know more once our staff update us. Sent by Donny at 08:26.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Name: Investigating & fixing

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Aaron at 16:06 - We can now confirm that all power in the Orbital Crescent area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now replaced.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Aaron at 15:38 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Orbital Crescent area, the estimated time of 18:30-19:30 hasn't changed. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. We'll update you with all of our progress, but if you need to speak to us, call our team on 08003163105 any time. If you would like more information about what we think the fault is; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse. Sent by Aaron at 15:15.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Aaron at 15:00 - Our staff are on the way to the Orbital Crescent area, and our estimated time frame to have power back on is currently between 18:30 and 19:30. However, this could change once our staff have assessed the local network.

We believe the power cuts you have been experiencing are caused by a fault with one of the cables. Developing faults can start very small, and so can be hard to detect but each power cut usually makes it easier to see, which means our specialist staff can detect it, and work on the repair to prevent further outages.

I'll let you know once our teams arrive.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Aaron at 15:29 - I appreciate your continued patience during the power cut today. If you're still off, please don't worry; work on the issue will continue along with SMS updates until everyone is back on. Steps.Message: Sent by Aaron at 14:57 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an underground network fault that initially interrupted power to 1895 properties. However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 256 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment. We're currently estimating power will be on between 17:30-18:30, but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.
